description_long: This object consists of two upright filing cabinets placed side by side, one green and one black. Each cabinet is rectangular with a flat top and features a pair of metallic handles on the black cabinet's front, which are likely used to pull out drawers. The green cabinet has a prominent white label holder with horizontal lines for writing, while the black cabinet has a similar label holder but it is less visible from the front. Both cabinets have circular finger holes near the bottom, commonly used for easy handling or pulling. The surfaces are smooth and matte, with the green and black colors dominating their respective cabinets. These cabinets are typically used for organizing and storing documents in folders, making them suitable for office or archive environments.
description: A pair of upright filing cabinets, one green and one black, each with metallic handles and label holders, designed for document storage.
description_view_0: Front view of the green cabinet, showing a flat, featureless green surface.
description_view_1: Side view of both cabinets, highlighting the white label holders and the slim profile.
description_view_2: Front view of the black cabinet, displaying two metallic handles and a hint of the green cabinet behind.
description_view_3: Top-down view showing the flat tops and the open slots of both cabinets.
